summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #658 in the moltis repo where the agent runner enters a 25-iteration dead loop when an LLM repeatedly calls a tool with invalid/empty arguments, wasting iterations without breaking the cycle.
summary.outcome: Implemented and merged PR #664 with three layered defenses: pre-dispatch schema validation (tool_arg_validator.rs), a ring-buffer loop detector with two-stage escalation (tool_loop_detector.rs), and runner integration on both streaming and non-streaming paths. All CI checks passed after fixing two additional Greptile P2 edge cases (false-positive intervention on mixed fail/success batches and stage-skip when nudge+strip fire in the same batch).

repo: Runner has two parallel code paths — non-streaming (around L895–L1460) and streaming (L1546–L1860) — that must be kept in sync for any dispatch-level changes.
AgentTool trait exposes parameters_schema() via crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:15, enabling pre-dispatch validation without touching individual tool execute() methods.
crates/chat/src/lib.rs contains a RunnerEvent forwarder match that must be updated when new RunnerEvent variants are added.
CLAUDE.md specifies no changelog entries and conventional commit format; also requires cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 for fmt/clippy.
The bd (Beads) issue tracker uses a Dolt database that must be attached per worktree via scripts/bd-worktree-attach.sh; it was offline in the evergreen-paper worktree.
CI uses Biome 2.4.6 specifically — the Homebrew default (2.4.11) is stricter and flags pre-existing violations in unrelated files, causing false CI failures.
scripts/local-validate.sh handles macOS-without-CUDA by stripping --all-features, avoiding llama-cpp-sys-2 build failures; it also publishes statuses directly to GitHub so CI gates can proceed.

code.finding: clear_strip_tools() must reset both intervention stage AND the ring buffer deque; clearing only the stage leaves the deque full and causes immediate re-escalation (oscillation bug).
JSON number type check for 'integer' schema type must accept floats where .fract() == 0.0 (e.g. 30.0), not just integer-typed JSON values.

Per-call return of PendingAction from record_call() is unreliable when multiple tool calls are batched — a success later in the batch resets the detector but the pending nudge flag from an earlier call in the same batch still fires. Solution: use consume_pending_action() polled once after the entire batch loop completes.
ToolCallStart event should be emitted only after schema validation passes, not before; calls rejected by validation must never emit ToolCallStart.
The streaming path accumulates args across chunks and finalizes them in a loop; adding debug!(args_str, 'finalizing tool call args') here aids future diagnosis of stream-accumulator edge cases.

workflow: Run scripts/local-validate.sh <pr-number> to publish local CI statuses to GitHub before pushing, preventing CI gates from timing out waiting for statuses.
When re-running failed CI jobs, first ensure local statuses are published — failed jobs often timed out waiting for local/* statuses rather than having real failures.
Greptile review bot posts P2 findings as PR comments and updates its overall score; resolving threads via gh api is required after fixing the underlying issues.
Adding new enum variants to RunnerEvent requires a grep across all crates for exhaustive match sites — crates/chat/src/lib.rs was the only one that needed updating in this case.
Using a 4-element result tuple (success, result, error_msg, rejected_flag) to carry rejection state through the existing pipeline avoids restructuring the AfterToolCall hook and event-emission code.

friction: bd (Beads) database was offline in the evergreen-paper worktree; could not file an issue tracker entry. Requires bd dolt pull or scripts/bd-worktree-attach.sh to fix.

Homebrew biome version (2.4.11) differs from CI-pinned version (2.4.6) and flags pre-existing violations in untouched files, causing misleading local failures. Had to manually download and install the pinned version.
just lint and just test fail in this environment due to a pre-existing CUDA toolkit absence (llama-cpp-sys-2); had to run cargo clippy and cargo test on individual crates instead.
local-validate.sh is a long-running background script (982s for macos-app alone); no live progress feedback without tailing the log file manually.
CI gates timed out waiting for local/* statuses on the first push; had to re-run failed jobs after publishing statuses via local-validate.sh.

Greptile required two rounds of review (3 P2s then 2 more P2s after the first fix) before reaching 5/5, with the second batch exposing a subtle batch-ordering race in the loop detector's action-reporting API.
open_items: bd database offline in evergreen-paper worktree — needs bd dolt pull or scripts/bd-worktree-attach.sh to reattach before filing issue tracker entries.
Pre-existing taplo TOML format issues in 5 files not touched by this PR — should be fixed in a separate cleanup commit.
tool_arg_validator is intentionally minimal (catches missing-required and top-level type mismatches only); upgrading to a full JSON Schema validator crate was explicitly deferred for a future PR.

Streaming-path integration tests for the reflex loop were added but coverage of the streaming path's consume_pending_action batch-ordering edge case (parallel fail+success) was only added for the non-streaming path — streaming equivalent may be worth adding.

No e2e/UI test covering the new ToolCallRejected and LoopInterventionFired events in the activity log (crates/web/src/assets/chat.js) — deferred pending a check of how the UI handles ToolCallEnd with no preceding ToolCallStart.
